purpose: Machine-readable authoring, review, privacy, and validation standard for future assessment items. This is not an item bank, runtime delivery API, or psychometric validation claim.

public_candidate_index_policy.description: Public candidate indexes use an explicit allowlist of safe selection metadata only. They must not expose governance workflow state, review state, field-test state, statistical state, scoring keys, reviewer data, approval history, private provenance, or statistical details.
public_candidate_index_policy.operational_item_status_when_safe: item_status may appear only as a canonical operational eligibility label and must not expose governance workflow_state/review_state or statistical lifecycle state.

item_id.validation: Stable opaque identifier unique across versions.

version.validation: Semantic or monotonic item version; immutable after administration.

package_id.validation: Must resolve to an approved curriculum package before operational use.

grade.validation: Target grade for the measured construct.

subject.validation: Subject label aligned to assessment framework.

domain.validation: Domain label aligned to package/framework domain.

construct.validation: One clear construct unless integrated_construct_rationale documents intentional integration.

integrated_construct_rationale.required_when: More than one construct is intentionally measured.

evidence_statement.validation: Specific observable evidence the response provides.

stimulus.validation: Text, image, audio, or interaction material needed to answer; no unrelated cultural knowledge.

prompt.validation: Authoring prompt source; must be clear, age-appropriate, and not tricky.

visible_prompt.validation: Text shown to learner; semantically equivalent to spoken_prompt.

spoken_prompt.validation: Audio script; semantically equivalent to visible_prompt and must not reveal the answer.

visual_requirements.validation: Required and prohibited visual affordances, alt text, contrast, and non-answer-revealing imagery.

audio_requirements.validation: Required narration, pronunciation, silence, replay, and no answer-revealing emphasis.

correct_answer.validation: Scoring key; never included in public candidate indexes.

acceptable_answers.validation: Equivalent acceptable responses for constructed and interaction items.

rubric.validation: Protected scoring rubric with full-credit and non-credit criteria.

partial_credit.validation: Partial-credit criteria or explicit disabled reason.

distractors.required_when: multiple_choice or multi_select; optional protected answer-option scoring metadata for matching/ordering/visual_interaction.

distractor_rationales.required_when: distractors are present; each distractor must identify the misconception represented.

prohibited_clues.validation: Known wording, formatting, audio, or visual clues that must be absent.

reading_level_target.validation: Grade-appropriate language target; math items minimize unrelated reading load.

assessment_role_eligibility.validation: Assessment roles where item may appear, subject to item_status and blueprint rules.

source_provenance.validation: Origin, authoring source, citations or generation provenance, licensing, and review trace.

change_history.validation: Immutable append-only changes with actor, timestamp, reason, materiality, and policy version.

statistical_metadata.validation: Derived from field-test/operational data; not authored into item content.

misconceptions.validation: Top-level item misconception taxonomy for the item; protected and non-public. Required when distractors or misconception mappings are present; option-level mappings live in misconception_for_each_distractor.

reviewer_notes.validation: Reviewer comments, concerns, decisions, and rationale notes; never included in public delivery or candidate indexes.

scoring_anchor_examples.validation: Protected constructed-response anchor examples for scorer calibration; required for constructed_response items when rubric or human scoring is used.

human_review_guidance.validation: Protected guidance for human review, escalation, and scorer calibration; required when automated scoring is insufficient or constructed-response scoring needs human judgment.

misconception_for_each_distractor.validation: Option-level protected mapping from each distractor identifier to one misconception taxonomy entry; required for multiple_choice and multi_select distractors.

single_correct_option.validation: Protected identifier of the exactly one correct option for multiple_choice items; must match one delivered option and must not be public.

option_ordering_policy.validation: Protected/randomization policy and clue checks for answer-option ordering; required for option-based items where ordering could cue an answer.

all_correct_options.validation: Protected list of all correct option identifiers for multi_select items; each identifier must match a delivered option.

select_all_instruction.validation: Learner-facing instruction that clearly indicates multi-select behavior without revealing the number or identity of correct options.

multi_select_scoring.validation: Deterministic protected scoring configuration for multi_select items.

property_validation.correct_option_weight: Numeric weight for each selected correct option.
property_validation.incorrect_option_penalty: Numeric penalty for each selected incorrect option; must be zero for all_or_nothing or additive unless explicitly justified for additive_with_incorrect_penalty.
property_validation.minimum_score: Numeric floor applied after raw scoring and before/with normalization as specified.
property_validation.maximum_score: Numeric ceiling; must be greater than or equal to minimum_score.
property_validation.omission_behavior: Explicit handling for unselected correct options, such as no_penalty, count_as_zero, or configured_omission_penalty.
property_validation.negative_scores_allowed: Boolean; if false, normalized and final scores must not be below minimum_score.
property_validation.normalization_rule: Exact transformation from raw score to reported item score, including rounding if any.

left_options.validation: Learner-facing left-side option set for matching items; option text must not reveal mappings.

right_options.validation: Learner-facing right-side option set for matching items; option text/order must not reveal mappings.

correct_mappings.validation: Protected deterministic scoring map for matching items; each required left option maps to the correct right option(s).

distractor_mappings_or_extra_options.validation: Protected matching-item distractor mappings or extra options and rationales; required when distractors/extra options are used.

accessibility_linearization.validation: Keyboard and screen-reader ordering/linearization for matching or complex layout items; must preserve construct equivalence without revealing answers.

ordered_elements.validation: Learner-facing elements to order; labels and metadata must not reveal the sequence.

correct_sequence.validation: Protected deterministic correct sequence for ordering items.

partial_order_rules.validation: Protected rules describing acceptable alternative or partial sequences and exact scoring behavior for ordering items.

drag_keyboard_equivalent.validation: Keyboard-accessible equivalent for drag/order interactions; must preserve response behavior without answer disclosure.

interaction_target_regions.validation: Learner-facing response geometry for visual interaction items, including target region identifiers and accessible labels; must not identify correct regions.

correct_interactions.validation: Protected deterministic scoring definition for visual interaction responses, keyed to target region identifiers and acceptable gestures/selections.

alternate_input_equivalent.validation: Accessible alternative input method for visual interactions; must be behavior-equivalent and non-answer-revealing.

authoring_principles: Measure one clear construct unless intentional integration is documented.
Use age-appropriate language and no trick wording.
Avoid accidental clues in wording, option length, formatting, visuals, audio, and ordering.
Multiple-choice and multi-select distractors must be plausible and misconception-based.
Math items must minimize unrelated reading load.
Visible and spoken prompts must be semantically equivalent.
Audio must not reveal the answer through wording, emphasis, pronunciation, pause, or sound effect.
Items must not require unrelated cultural knowledge.
Forms must not contain duplicate or near-duplicate operational items.
Protected scoring fields must not be exposed through public candidate indexes.
Top-level misconceptions define protected item-level misconception taxonomy; misconception_for_each_distractor defines protected option-level mappings.

Multi-select items must provide deterministic protected multi_select_scoring semantics, including scoring mode, weights, penalties, floors, ceilings, omission behavior, negative-score policy, normalization, and partial-credit flag.

Public candidate indexes must use only the explicit allowlist and must not expose review_state, workflow_state, field_test_state, statistical_state, reviewer notes, approval history, private provenance, scoring keys, or statistical parameters.

validation_rules.description: All always_required fields must be present and non-empty.
question_type must be one of the defined question_type_vocabulary values.
All conditional requirements for the selected question_type must be satisfied.
Items measuring more than one construct require integrated_construct_rationale and curriculum-review approval.
Prompt and stimulus language must be age-appropriate and free of trick wording.
Math items with high unrelated reading load require revision or documented rationale.
No accidental answer clues may appear in prompt, stimulus, options, visuals, audio, or metadata delivered to learners.
Every distractor must have a rationale and represented misconception.
spoken_prompt and audio_requirements must not reveal the answer.
visible_prompt and spoken_prompt must be semantically equivalent.
Items requiring unrelated cultural knowledge require revision or documented construct relevance.
Operational forms must not include duplicate or near-duplicate items measuring the same construct through substantially identical stimulus or answer path.
Protected scoring, answer, rubric, rationale, reviewer, and statistical fields must not be classified as public_delivery_safe or emitted in public candidate indexes.
item_status must use the canonical item-status vocabulary.
Items used for operational mastery decisions must have item_status validated_operational.
Every field referenced by required metadata, visibility lists, candidate-index policy, or conditional question-type requirements must have a field_definitions entry.

Multi-select items must include protected multi_select_scoring with scoring_mode, correct_option_weight, incorrect_option_penalty, minimum_score, maximum_score, omission_behavior, negative_scores_allowed, normalization_rule, and partial_credit_enabled.

Public candidate indexes must include only explicit_public_allowlist fields and must not include review_state, workflow_state, field_test_state, statistical_state, reviewer_notes, statistical metadata, private provenance, approval history, or scoring data.
